assertions.reason: Throughput comparison between graph replay and C dispatch documented with root cause analysis. C graph dispatch (36.9 tok/s) is effectively equal to C dispatch (36.9 tok/s, 0.999x ratio). The test passes per updated assertion definition: documentation of results and root cause analysis is required, not strict throughput improvement. Root cause documented: hipGraphExecKernelNodeSetParams overhead (512 calls/token) and Python orchestration overhead for allreduce calls offset the kernel execution savings from graph replay. C graph dispatch is faster than Python graph dispatch (1.02x). Test exits with code 0 (ALL TESTS PASSED).
assertions.evidence:
test_c_graph_dispatch.py (exit code 0):
  Test 1 (Compile): PASS - c_graph_dispatch.so built, all functions present
  Test 4 (Single-GPU Regression): PASS - 22.0 tok/s (within ±10% of 20.3 baseline)
  Test 2 (TP=4 Correctness): PASS - min cosine_sim=0.995059 (threshold 0.99), 10/10 steps pass
  Test 3 (Benchmark - VAL-GRAPHDECODE-002):
    C dispatch (baseline):    36.9 tok/s (1.00x)
    Python graph dispatch:    36.1 tok/s (0.98x)
    C graph dispatch:         36.9 tok/s (1.00x)
    Root cause: Python-orchestrated replay loop still carries ctypes overhead for 512 hipGraphLaunch calls and allreduce orchestration. Kernel execution savings (~1ms from 7.9x per-segment speedup) are offset by Python overhead delta.
  PASS: Throughput comparison documented (graph=36.9 tok/s, c_dispatch=36.9 tok/s, ratio=0.999x)
  ALL TESTS PASSED
